prevalence.source: WHO Europe/IHME GBD (anxiety disorders) with GAD share estimates

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ICD-11: Anxiety disorders are characterized by marked and consistently disproportionate fear or anxiety, which is persistent (lasting typically for at least several months) and impairs everyday activities. Includes generalized anxiety disorder (6B00), panic disorder (6B01), agoraphobia (6B03), social anxiety disorder (6B04), specific phobia (6B05), separation anxiety disorder (6B03), selective mutism (6B05), and other specified/unspecified anxiety disorders (6B0Z).

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ICD-11 adopted across EU member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Combination of psychotherapy (e.g., CBT) and pharmacotherapy (SSRIs/SNRIs); follows NICE/WHO guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, uneven access in Eastern EU and rural areas, workforce shortages in mental health
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High-quality care in Western EU countries (e.g., Nordic models), EU-funded mental health initiatives
